extraction_rule: Stable row key. Do not change.
Source trial.
Registry id.
Treatment as named in the paper.
Human-readable component role.
Inhaler device. Drives commensurability.
1 for the commensurable BGF family; 0 for the BUD/FORM DPI arm.
ICS component dose. THE dose-varying component (320 vs 160). 0 if absent. Metered-dose labeling; delivered equivalents are the same product.
LABA component dose. Constant across active arms (Emax will not separate from ED50; report effect at this dose). 0 if absent.
LAMA component dose. Constant across active arms. 0 if absent.
Dosing frequency.
Trial duration. Note ETHOS 52 wk vs KRONOS 24 wk for the time axis and for the exacerbation rate window.
Eligibility and randomization ratio. Transitivity flag: ETHOS requires >=1 prior exacerbation, KRONOS does not.
Randomized to this arm.
mITT analysis N for the exacerbation outcome. Denominator for rates.
Analysis N for the FEV1 value. In ETHOS this is the 4-h PFT SUB-STUDY, smaller than n_mitt_exac. In KRONOS it is the mITT.
Exact FEV1 endpoint definition. Keep per-trial wording.
Timepoint or averaging window. Differs between trials; record both so the model can align or treat as separate times.
PER-ARM least-squares mean CHANGE FROM BASELINE (absolute), NOT the between-arm difference. Pull each arm's own value from the lung-function figure/table.
Standard error of the per-arm LS mean. *Provide SE, or the 95% CI (next two fields), or SD+N; any one suffices and the analyst derives the rest.
Lower 95% CI of the per-arm LS mean change. SE = (hi-lo)/(2*1.96).
Upper 95% CI of the per-arm LS mean change.
Per-arm SD if reported instead of SE; with n_fev1, SE = SD/sqrt(n).
Exacerbation outcome definition (moderate or severe).
Number of moderate/severe exacerbations in the arm. Best paired with patient-years for a Poisson/negative-binomial rate.
Total exposure time at risk in the arm. Enables a log-offset rate model (the reviewer's negative-binomial point).
Adjusted annualized rate, if events/person-years are not separately reported.
Lower 95% CI of the annualized rate.
Upper 95% CI of the annualized rate.
Patients with >=1 moderate/severe exacerbation. Binomial fallback if only proportions are available.
Denominator for exac_pts_with_ge1 (usually = n_mitt_exac).
Baseline mean age. Transitivity/balance check.
Percent male.
Baseline post-bronchodilator FEV1.
Baseline FEV1 percent predicted (severity).
Baseline blood eosinophils. KEY effect modifier for the ICS component; lets the analyst show balance rather than assume it.
Percent with eosinophils >=300 cells/uL, if the mean is not given.
Mean moderate/severe exacerbations in the prior year. Transitivity flag (ETHOS vs KRONOS differ).
Percent current smokers.
Citation the FEV1 value came from (e.g. ETHOS lung-function sub-study paper).
Citation the exacerbation value came from (primary outcome paper).
Citation for the baseline/covariate values.
Specific table or figure number per value. Add page if helpful.
Who extracted the row.
Extraction date.
Pre-filled with structural notes; append anything unusual (e.g. value read from a figure, imputation, discrepancy).
